sources.focusRingRotationDeg: Canon IAS Cine Servo family pattern: CN7x17 KAS S confirmed 180° (canon-europe.com), CN20x50 IAS H confirmed 180° (canon-europe.com). CN30x40 is same Cine Servo family. No explicit spec sheet statement found for CN30x40.
sources.gearPitch: Servo lens; no gear pitch applicable

lenses.description: The 30x successor to the 50-1000, both wider and longer at nearly the same size and weight, reaching 1800mm with its built-in 1.5x extender. The aperture holds T5.0 to 560mm and ramps to T10.8 at 1200mm. It covers Super 35 natively and full frame with the extender, with 8K-grade optics, an eleven-blade iris and focus-breathing compensation. The native RF mount adds distortion correction and real-time metadata for virtual production, driven by a removable e-Xs servo unit for broadcast or cine control.

sources.hasFocusBreathing: Canon CN-E FF 20-50mm: minimal focus breathing, cine-optimized full-frame zoom (tier-1)
sources.imageCircleMm: Canon does not publish exact imageCircleMm for CN-E FF 20-50mm — Tim: zoek spec sheet

lenses.description: One of Canon's first full-frame cinema zooms, a constant-T2.4 large-format zoom in the Flex Zoom series. The T-stop stays constant across the range, with an eleven-blade iris, 8K-grade optics and the warm, colour-matched Cinema EOS look, and it is parfocal, holding focus through the zoom. It pairs with the 45-135mm T2.4 as a matched full-frame set, sharing front diameter and gears for fast swaps. The Flex design also allows relay-kit reconfiguration between full-frame and Super 35 coverage.

sources.hasFocusBreathing: Canon CN-E FF 45-135mm: minimal focus breathing, cine-optimized full-frame zoom (tier-1)
sources.isParfocal: Canon CN-E FF 45-135mm T2.4: parfocal confirmed (tier-1)
sources.imageCircleMm: Canon does not publish exact imageCircleMm for CN-E FF 45-135mm — Tim: zoek spec sheet

lenses.description: The telephoto half of Canon's first full-frame cinema zoom pair, a constant-T2.4 large-format Flex Zoom matched to the 20-50mm T2.4. The aperture stays constant across the range, with an eleven-blade iris, 8K-grade colour-matched optics and a parfocal design that holds focus through the zoom. Shared front diameter and gear positions make swaps with the 20-50 quick, and the Flex design allows relay-kit reconfiguration between formats. It suits full-frame narrative and documentary work needing a bright, consistent zoom.
